role_delineation_check: The Social Worker is responsible for selecting the home health agency and coordinating the nursing schedule, while the Navigator focuses on verifying the accuracy of Arthur's contact information in Atlantis, educating his daughter on the transition process, and alerting the Social Worker when a scheduled home visit is missed.
narrative_summary: Arthur, an 86-year-old gentleman living with dementia and a history of frequent falls, was preparing to transition home with the support of his daughter. While the daughter felt prepared after the Navigator explained the transition process and the role of the Medical Assistant, the plan faltered on the first day when the expected home health nurse did not arrive. The Navigator acted as a vital liaison by documenting the daughter's distress in Atlantis and immediately alerting the Social Worker to the gap in care, ensuring the facility team was aware of the home safety risk.
demographic_audit_note: I performed a side-by-side audit of the facility face sheet and the Atlantis record; I discovered that Arthur's apartment number was missing from the system and his daughter’s mobile number had two transposed digits. I corrected these details in Atlantis to ensure the home team and the Medical Assistant would have the correct location and contact information for the first visit.
home_vs_ltc_goal: The Social Worker confirmed that Arthur will be discharging to his private residence with his daughter as the primary caregiver.
v_card_flyer_status: I sat with Arthur's daughter and helped her save the Healing Partners contact information into her phone as a V-Card so she would recognize our caller ID; I also provided the program flyer and explained that this number is for non-clinical transition support.
pre_dc_pulse_call: I reached the daughter 24 hours prior to discharge; she expressed high anxiety regarding Arthur's fall risk but confirmed she was ready for him to return home, provided the home health agency arrived as promised.
atlantis_final_sync: Following a verbal update from the Social Worker, I recorded the confirmed discharge date for Thursday and scheduled the Medical Assistant for the initial home visit on Friday morning.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The Social Worker informed me that a home health agency had accepted the referral and was set to begin services on Thursday afternoon; I documented this in Atlantis and updated the daughter on the timeline.

During my final bedside visit, the daughter shared her concern that she had not yet received a call from the agency to confirm a specific arrival window; I noted this concern in Atlantis and asked the Social Worker for a status update.
Arthur was discharged home on Thursday afternoon as planned, and I confirmed the daughter was present to receive him.

On Friday morning, the daughter called me in a state of distress stating the home health nurse never arrived for the scheduled intake; I documented the no-show in Atlantis and alerted the Social Worker that the transition plan had stalled.

format_2_triples.situation: The daughter was anxious about recognizing calls from the care team during the first 24 hours home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: I helped the daughter save our contact information into her phone and verified she understood how to identify our caller ID.

format_2_triples.situation: The home health agency failed to appear for the first scheduled visit after Arthur's discharge.
format_2_triples.action_taken: I documented the service gap in Atlantis and alerted the Social Worker to the missed visit so they could intervene with the agency.

format_2_triples.situation: The daughter asked for the specific time the home health nurse would arrive to check Arthur's vitals.
format_2_triples.action_taken: I explained that the Social Worker manages the nursing schedule and that I would request a status update on her behalf.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: After hearing from the daughter that the nurse had not arrived, the Navigator records the communication in Arthur's Atlantis log and waits for the Social Worker to respond to the notification.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the boundary that the Social Worker owns the relationship with the agency and the Navigator's role is to document and report communication gaps.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator contacts the Social Worker to relay the daughter's report of a no-show and asks if there is an updated arrival time they can share with the family to de-escalate their anxiety.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This demonstrates active liaison work by seeking information from the correct internal source without attempting to manage the vendor directly.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator calls the home health agency's intake coordinator to find out why the nurse missed the appointment and attempts to coordinate a new visit time for that afternoon.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This violates the no-vendor rule and oversteps into the Social Worker's responsibility for managing the agency's performance and schedule.
